data.title: Nikkei Rises 0.7%, Led by Electronics, Machinery Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks ended higher as concerns about borrowing costs abated. Electronics and machinery stocks led the gains. Advantest climbed 5.3% and Mitsubishi Electric gained 3.6%. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.7% to 37053.10. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield fell 2.5 basis points to 1.520%. Besides U.S. tariff news, investors are focusing on any developments related to Japanese monetary policy ahead of the Bank of Japan's policy meeting next week. USD/JPY is at 148.66, up from 147.81 as of Thursday 5 p.m. Eastern time. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0652 GMT - Japanese stocks ended higher as concerns about borrowing costs abated. Electronics and machinery stocks led the gains. Advantest climbed 5.3% and Mitsubishi Electric gained 3.6%. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.7% to 37053.10. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield fell 2.5 basis points to 1.520%. Besides U.S. tariff news, investors are focusing on any developments related to Japanese monetary policy ahead of the Bank of Japan's policy meeting next week. USD/JPY is at 148.66, up from 147.81 as of Thursday 5 p.m. Eastern time. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0647 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi fell 0.3% to close at 2566.36, dragged by battery and steel stocks. The index edged up 0.1% for the week. Foreign and institutional investors were net sellers in Friday's session, as anxiety about an escalating global trade war weighed on sentiment. Battery maker Samsung SDI fell 6.2% after a new rights issue plan, as its bigger local peer LG Energy Solution lost 4.1%. Steelmakers Posco Holdings and Hyundai Steel slid 2.6% and 4.0%, respectively. Meanwhile, biosimilar company Celltrion rose 0.8% after a share cancellation plan. USD/KRW settled flat at 1,453.80 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield was up 0.6 basis point at 2.776%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0637 GMT - Citi Research remains bearish on eurozone government bonds in the near term, with yield spreads over German Bunds likely tightening only in the more medium term, says strategist Aman Bansal in a note. While the recent spike in yields could bolster demand, headwinds might eventually emerge from the higher funding costs, he says in a note. "The near-term demand boost from the Bund-led increase in eurozone government bond yields might give way to more persistent headwinds from tighter financing conditions and progressively increasing funding costs." This adds to already prevailing downside factors, among them the imminent U.S. tariffs, in particular, he says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0605 GMT - The 10-year German Bund yield could trade close to 3% in the coming three to six months, says SEB Research's Jussi Hiljanen in a note. The chief strategist forecasts the 10-year Bund yield to trade mostly between 2.50% and 3.00% in the coming months, above SEB Research's previous target range of 2.20%-2.60%. The revision follows Germany's plans for fiscal expansion. Further rebalancing of positions could push yields higher, but several factors, including further European Central Bank rate cuts, should cap the upside in long yields to around 3% for now, Hiljanen says. The 10-year Bund yield closed 4 basis points lower at 2.856% on Thursday, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0601 GMT - The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield is likely to settle at a lower range in coming months than previously expected by SEB Research, says chief strategist Jussi Hiljanen in a note. SEB Research now expects the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield to trade in a 4.00-4.50% range in the coming 3-6 months versus its previous forecast range of 4.20-5.00%. "This revision reflects our view that markets are now less likely to fade Federal Reserve rate cut expectations as concerns about negative tariff effects are factored into outlooks," Hiljanen says. The 10-year Treasury yield trades at 4.289%, up 1.3 basis points, according to LSEG. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: South Korea reports first foot-and-mouth disease case in nearly two years
data.description:
South Korea has confirmed a foot-and-mouth disease case in a cattle farm, the first such outbreak in nearly two years, the agriculture ministry said on Friday.

The case, the first in the country since May 2023, was reported in a cattle farm in the southwestern Jeollanam-do province, the ministry said in a statement, adding that about 180 cattle in the farm would be culled.

The government has since raised the alert, stepping up disinfecting efforts in the region, according to the ministry.

Foot-and-mouth disease causes fever and mouth blisters in cloven-hoofed ruminants such as cattle, swine, sheep and goats.

data.title: Trump-backed World Liberty Financial (WLFI) Completes $590M Token Sale
data.description:
World Liberty Financial (WLFI), the Donald Trump-backed crypto project, has closed its token sale after raising approximately $590 million.

The project's raise of $590 million would put it in the top-10 list of token raises, according to data curated by ICODrops. To date, the largest token sale is EOS, which raised $4.21 billion.

EOS is a blockchain platform developed by Block.one, which later founded Bullish, CoinDesk's owner.

On stage at Consensus 2025 in Hong Kong, WLFI co-founder Zak Folkman credited Tron's Justin Sun with the success of the project's token sale.

After WLFI first launched its sale, its critics called the momentum sluggish. But this changed after Sun invested $30 million into it in November 2024 and later invested more.

"When we were launching this project, it was a very heated time," Folkman said during Consensus. "There was a lot of scrutiny on our project due to who was involved."

This meant that traditional crypto VCs would not touch the token.

"[Sun] saw that regardless of the outcome, this project is a monumental move forward for the entire crypto community," Folkman added during the Consensus panel.

Rules around WLFI's token sale mean that the token was only available to accredited investors and can't be transferred or publicly sold on exchanges. A date has not been set for an exchange listing.

data.title: AI's Lead Over Crypto for VC Dollars Increased in Q1'25, But Does This Race Really Matter?
data.description:
Crypto venture funding in the U.S. clocked in at approximately $861 million for the first three months of 2025, but was dwarfed by artificial intelligence's nearly $20 billion haul, according to data provided by Pitchbook, showing how investors continue to pivot towards AI.

Data shows that investors closed 795 deals in the U.S in AI from January to March, with blockbuster deals like Databricks' $15.3 billion round and Anthropic's $2 billion raise dominating headlines.

Crypto's largest blockbuster deal, in comparison, was Abu Dhabi's MGX, with a $2 billion investment into Binance – the first institutional placement in the crypto exchange. Other deals of note include a $82 million raise from payment infrastructure company Mesh, ETF issuer Bitwise's $70 million round, and digital asset bank Sygnum's $58 million offering.

Prior reporting by Pitchbook shows that AI startups attracted one-third of global VC investment in 2024, totaling $131.5 billion, with nearly a quarter of new startups being an AI company across 4,318 VC deals, compared to crypto's $4.9 billion across just 706 deals.

Analysis: Has AI stolen crypto's venture dollars?

Blockbuster rounds from VCs in the AI space and headline-grabbing antics, such as OpenAI's Sam Altman seeking trillions, and AI's rise from technological novelty to household name thanks to transformer models, would make one think that there's suddenly an investor preference for one over the other.

Historically, all data shows that VCs have generally favored AI over crypto, with AI and machine learning attracting consistent funding that's expanded exponentially, according to Statista data, growing from $670 million in 2011 to $36 billion in 2020.

There's only been one year where crypto beat AI for funding, and that comes with a caveat: narrower AI categorizations, like ABI Research's $22.3 billion AI estimate in 2021, suggest crypto briefly outpaced AI funding during the bullish crypto cycle before AI funding surged again to over $100 billion by 2024.

Keep in mind that all of this ignores crypto-native quirks like airdrops, which put fresh capital in the hands of users and, in turn, pump the token price, inflating the size of projects' treasuries.

A recent report from Dragonfly found that between 2020 and 2024, the 11 largest airdrops generated $7 billion. This won't close the gap between AI and crypto, but it shows that there are more ways to get a dollar than traditional venture capital.

data.title: Daimler Truck to Cut Costs at Mercedes-Benz Trucks Unit After Earnings Decline
data.description:

(06:30 GMT) *Daimler Truck Says Works Council Talks Have Started to Define Concrete, Sustainable Measures
(06:54 GMT) Daimler Truck to Cut Costs at Mercedes-Benz Trucks Unit After Earnings Decline

By Pierre Bertrand

Daimler Truck said it aims to cut costs at its Mercedes-Benz Trucks unit in Europe by more than a billion euros ($1.09 billion) after posting a decline in fourth-quarter earnings amid weak European demand.

The German trucks and buses manufacturer said it plans to achieve the savings at the business segment by 2030 at the latest. It added that the company has started talks with the works council.

The move comes as Daimler said it expects an operationally stable 2025, compared with last year, but that the market for heavy-duty trucks in Europe would remain weak this year.

"After a record year in 2023, the company's financial performance in 2024 was impacted by a slowdown in demand in key markets, particularly affecting Mercedes-Benz Trucks in Europe," Daimler Truck said.

The company achieved 1.10 billion euros in fourth-quarter adjusted earnings before interest and taxes, compared with 1.56 billion a year prior.

Revenue for the quarter came to 14.35 billion euros, a 4% on-year decrease.

The company, which in January said it sold 12% fewer vehicles last year than in 2023 due to declines at its Truck North America, Mercedes-Benz and Trucks Asia segments, said it would propose a flat 1.90 euro dividend for 2024.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: OCBC Buys Back Shares Worth Nearly SG$6 Million
data.description:
Oversea-Chinese Banking bought back 350,000 shares in the open market on Thursday for SG$5.8 million or SG$16.51 a piece, a filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The bank has been authorized to buy back 225.0 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has repurchased 16.4 million shares.

data.title: China Resources Land's Contracted Sales, Recurring Revenue Rise in February; Shares Jump 5%
data.description:
China Resources Land's contracted sales rose 47% year over year to 13.5 billion yuan in February, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Contracted gross floor area increased 28% to 585,000 square meters, the filing said.

Recurring revenue increased 13% to 4.24 billion yuan.

Shares rose nearly 5% during Friday's afternoon trading.

data.title: Market Chatter: Niantic Offloads Pokemon Go Unit to Scopely for $3.5 Billion
data.description:
Niantic will sell its game business, including Pokemon Go, to US-based Scopely for $3.5 billion as it shifts focus to AI and 3-D mapping, Nikkei Asia reported Thursday.

The company will spin off its geospatial and augmented reality platform into Niantic Spatial, with shares allocated to existing Niantic stockholders and Scopely. Niantic founder John Hanke will lead Niantic Spatial as CEO, the report said.

The deal values Niantic at $3.85 billion, including a cash distribution, according to the report.

Niantic Spatial will retain AR games such as Ingress Prime and continue offering geospatial services to outside clients. More than half of Niantic's employees will move to Niantic Spatial, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: EuroSports Global Repurchases 29,900 Shares
data.description:
EuroSports Global repurchased 65,000 shares on the market for SG$9,154 or SG$0.14 apiece on Thursday, a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange said.

The company has so far repurchased 1.8 million shares out of the 24,552,810 shares it is authorized to repurchase under its current mandate.

data.title: French finance minister labels tariffs situation as an "idiotic war"
data.description:
Key points:
  • Trump's threat to escalate tariffs on French liquor is not a surprise
  • It's an "idiotic war"

Idiotic or not, they're dragged into it and have to make do with the situation I guess. In case you missed it: Trump threatens heavy tariffs on French wines and EU alcohol

Battery maker Samsung SDI fell 6.2% after a new rights issue plan, as its bigger local peer LG Energy Solution lost 4.1%. data.title: BMW Expects Flat Earnings This Year as Headwinds Continue
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

BMW expects to report slight sales growth this year but said earnings would remain flat as it faces headwinds from continuing challenges in China and costs related to tariffs and its supply chain.

European automakers are contending with a demand shift in China as consumers in the world's largest car market move toward local brands that are offering deep discounts, while a struggling Chinese economy has also forced a downturn in luxury spending.

The dynamics have hit the higher-end German manufacturers like BMW especially hard as it derives around a third of group earnings from the country.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: Markets come up for air but tariff threats lurk
data.description:
MARKETS COME UP FOR AIR BUT TARIFF THREATS LURK

Global stocks caught some much-needed relief on Friday after a week of heavy battering, momentarily putting aside worries over trade wars when top U.S. Senate Democrat Chuck Schumer signalled his party would provide the votes to avert a government shutdown.

Traders reacted quickly to send stock futures higher: Nasdaq futures jumped more than 1% while S&P 500 futures climbed 0.8%, relatively big moves for Asian hours.

MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) rose 1%.

Europe similarly looked set for a strong opening, with EUROSTOXX 50 futures gaining 0.3% and DAX futures tacking on 0.5%.

The market's touchiness towards news that had rarely stirred much reaction in the past suggested just how jittery investors had become.

"If you're already in a haunted house and you're already rattled, then every little mouse will scare you," said Vishnu Varathan, Mizuho's head of macro research for Asia ex-Japan.

In recent years markets have been largely unfazed by headlines surrounding U.S. government shutdowns, given that lawmakers routinely pass temporary spending bills to keep the government operating while they finish their work.

But going into the week's end, global markets were reeling over fears of an impending U.S. recession and an escalating global trade war.

Traders in London will wake up to the release of UK growth estimates for January due early in Europe's day. Expectations are for the economy to have grown 0.1% on a monthly basis, a slight slowdown from December's 0.4%.

It's worth noting that Britain's relatively measured approach to U.S. trade ructions and the largely balanced trade position between the two have benefited the pound, which was on track for a second straight weekly gain.

Over in the U.S., the University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers will be the key item to watch on Friday, given how U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff salvos are rattling businesses and consumers alike.

Meanwhile, Chinese equities have emerged as an unlikely sanctuary for global investors seeking shelter from all the uncertainty in the United States.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index is up 17% since Trump returned to the White House in January, compared with a drop of about 9% in the S&P 500 .

Stocks in mainland China and Hong Kong surged again on Friday, led by consumer shares, after a northern Chinese city announced plans to boost birth rates. Investors are also awaiting a press conference next Monday by Chinese officials from the top planning agency and elsewhere for additional measures to enhance domestic consumption.

Key developments that could influence markets on Friday:

- UK GDP estimate (January)

- UK industrial output (January)

- U.S. University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers (March)

(Rae Wee)

*****

data.title: Haitong Unitrust Files for Listing of 1 Billion Yuan Bonds
data.description:
Haitong Unitrust International Finance Leasing has applied to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange to list and deal in 1 billion yuan of 3% bonds maturing in 2028 through unit Haitong UT Brilliant, a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The bonds will be issued under the $1 billion guaranteed medium-term bond program by way of debt issues to professional investors.

The listing is expected to become effective on or around Friday, March 14.

data.title: Intrum Reaches Settlement with Creditor Group
data.description:
Intrum said Friday it reached a settlement agreement with a minority group of 2025 noteholders who are opposing its prepackaged Chapter 11 reorganization plan in the US.

As part of the deal, the 2025 Ad Hoc Group will withdraw all appeals related to the Chapter 11 cases, the pre-packaged plan, the amended medium-term notes, and the Swedish reorganization process, and back the Swedish credit management company's recapitalization transaction. In exchange, Intrum will pay certain advisor fees of the ad hoc group six months after the recapitalization's completion.

The settlement agreement requires approval by the US Bankruptcy Court and the administrator of the Swedish Reorganisation Process.

data.title: Iron Ore Rises on China Stimulus Hopes
data.description:
Iron ore futures climbed above $102 per tonne in mid-March, hitting a two-week high on expectations of additional stimulus measures from China, the world’s top consumer of the metal.

Optimism grew as top government officials in Beijing prepared to hold a press conference on Monday to discuss policies aimed at boosting consumption.

Chinese steelmakers also ramped up production during the peak construction season, driving hot metal output up to 2.31 million tons and further lifting iron ore demand.

However, the Chinese government reaffirmed its commitment to limiting crude steel production due to concerns of overcapacity among blast furnaces and mills, a move expected to reduce steel output by 50 million tons annually.

Adding to trade tensions, Vietnam increased dumping levies on Chinese steel, while South Korea, Brazil, and Chile signaled plans to implement similar measures.

data.title: Jawala Trims Attributable Loss in Fiscal H1
data.description:
Jawala trimmed its net attributable loss to equity holders for the half ended Jan 31 to 17 million ringgit from 2.6 million ringgit a year earlier, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Loss per share narrowed to 0.0147 ringgit compared with 0.0219 in the comparable period.

Revenue surged to 7.5 million ringgit from 141,000 ringgit a year earlier, mainly due to higher logging activities.

data.title: Shanghai Sanyou Medical Completes Private Placement
data.description:
Shanghai Sanyou Medical completed its private placement to fund an asset acquisition and associated transactions.

The orthopedic products maker has submitted the required documentation to the Shanghai Stock Exchange and will proceed with registering the newly issued shares, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the company rose 1% in recent trade.

data.title: BMW net profit slumps as weak China sales, brake problems plague earnings
data.description:
BMW's net profit crashed by over a third in 2024 to 7.68 billion euros ($8.32 billion), in line with market expectations, after a combination of weak sales in China and Germany as well as delivery hold-ups because of problems with a brake dented performance.

The premium carmaker expects its earnings margin for cars to be 5-7% in 2025, at best a slight increase from last year's 6.3%, it said on Friday, anticipating intensifying trade wars and the continuation of tough competition in China.

Its forecast takes into account all tariffs imposed by March 12, the carmaker said, which include increased tariffs on all U.S. steel and aluminium imports and a 25% duty on some vehicles from Mexico, including BMW's.

The group proposed a higher payout ratio of 36.7%, among the highest in its history, consisting of a dividend of 4.32 euros per preferred share for 2024, still down from 6.02 euros paid out for the previous year.

($1 = 0.9221 euros)

data.title: Market Chatter: China Officials Meet Walmart Following Reports It Seeks Supplier Price Cuts
data.description:
Chinese commerce officials met with Walmart following reports the US retail chain has asked suppliers from China to slash their prices to offset tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump, Global Times reported Thursday.

Walmart's request for Chinese suppliers to slash prices puts supply chains at risk and will harm Chinese and US interests as well as US customers, the report said, citing state-affiliated media outlet Yuyuantantian.

"We will continue to work closely with them (suppliers) to find the best way forward during these uncertain times," Reuters reported separately, quoting a spokesperson for Walmart.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Miami Businesses Turning to High-Performance Website Design to Outpace Competitors, Digital Silk Study Finds
data.description:
Miami, Florida--(Newsfile Corp. - March 14, 2025) - Digital Silk, an award-winning agency specializing in high-performance website design, branding, and digital marketing, has found that Miami businesses investing in modern website design are seeing greater engagement, improved user retention, and stronger brand authority. A recent Digital Silk study analyzing web performance trends reveals that companies prioritizing sleek, fast, and conversion-driven websites are gaining a significant competitive advantage in the digital landscape.

Miami Businesses Turning to High-Performance Website Design to Outpace Competitors, Digital Silk Study Finds

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/10732/244480_641ed80e148bbb5d_001full.jpg

According to a report by Statista, U.S. e-commerce sales alone are projected to surpass $1.3 trillion in 2025, emphasizing the growing demand for seamless online experiences. With Miami emerging as a hub for innovation and entrepreneurship, businesses across industries-from hospitality and retail to tech startups-are investing in web design strategies that prioritize speed, aesthetics, and functionality.

Why Miami Businesses Are Prioritizing High-Performance Websites

A website is often a company's most valuable digital asset. With competition heating up across industries, Miami brands are leveraging modern web design techniques to gain an edge. The key elements driving this transformation include:

  • Advanced UX/UI Design: Ensuring intuitive navigation and a seamless user experience.

  • Mobile Optimization: Over 60% of web traffic now comes from mobile devices, making responsiveness a priority.

  • SEO & Speed Enhancements: Faster load times improve rankings and conversions.

  • Conversion-Optimized Pages: Engaging visuals and strategic CTAs guide visitors toward action.

  • Security & Performance Upgrades: SSL encryption, optimized hosting, and clean coding enhance both trust and functionality.

Miami's Digital Transformation: Results-Driven Website Strategies

Miami businesses are seeing firsthand how strategic website design can transform their online presence. Companies investing in a high-performance website report:

  • Higher Engagement: A well-structured, visually compelling site keeps visitors engaged longer.

  • Increased Conversion Rates: Optimized landing pages and call-to-action placements lead to more inquiries and sales.

  • Stronger Brand Authority: A polished, professional website builds credibility and brand trust.

"Today's consumers expect fast, visually appealing, and easy-to-navigate websites. A business's online presence can either drive customer loyalty or push potential clients toward competitors," said says Stephanie Sharlow - VP, Client Partner Digital Silk

The Future of Digital Innovation in Miami

As Miami's business landscape evolves, so do the expectations for digital performance. AI-driven personalization, immersive brand storytelling, and interactive website features are becoming essential tools for success.

With a commitment to creating cutting-edge digital experiences, Digital Silk is at the forefront of this transformation, helping Miami businesses stay ahead of the competition with high-performing, conversion-driven websites.
